_COMMENT_BF.full_name: Legacy Comment Block with Carriage Return
_COMMENT_BF.description: Used to provide carriage-return/page-feed functionality in Mark VI and early Mark VIe controllers
notes: Deprecated legacy block, replaced by _COMMENT
Hidden by default in ToolboxST
Updated MAX_TIME to MAXTIME
Added Note to clarify when AT_TIME goes True
Corrected parameter name from mCurTime to CURTIME

TRAN_DLY.description: Collects input samples each scan and stores them in an array. Outputs delayed values based on specified delay.

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Next value to be entered into the list

inputs.name: N_DELAY
inputs.description: Size of the array that defines the LIST variable

outputs.name: OLD
outputs.description: Second oldest value stored in LIST array

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Newest or Nth stored value in the list as specified by N_DELAY

states.name: LIST[]
states.description: Storage array for the delay list

states.name: SLIST[]
states.description: Storage array for the delay list status

notes: This block is not recommended for use in any new controller applications (ControlST V05.02 or later). Refer to Legacy Category Blocks.
Block propagates quality status if status operations are enabled.
The array size of the variable connected to LIST[] pin should be greater than or equal to N_DELAY
SLIST pin is only present on status version of the block

ABS.description: Takes a single input and produces a single output
notes: Part of Single Input/Single Output block category

ADD.description: Uses multiple inputs to produce an output
notes: Part of Relational block category
Only partial block information visible in image
Shows block being added via Library View

ANALOG_ALARM_1.description: Analog alarm block with multiple setpoints and hysteresis

outputs.name: IN
outputs.description: The Analog Alarm Variable associated to the block

outputs.name: INH
outputs.description: The master inhibit flag for the analog alarm

outputs.name: H
outputs.description: The Hi flag for the analog alarm

outputs.name: HH
outputs.description: The Hi Hi flag for the analog alarm

outputs.name: HHH
outputs.description: The Hi Hi Hi flag for the analog alarm

outputs.name: L
outputs.description: The Low flag for the analog alarm

outputs.name: LL
outputs.description: The Low Low flag for the analog alarm

outputs.name: LLL
outputs.description: The Low Low Low flag for the analog alarm

states.name: H_SP
states.description: The Hi Setpoint for the analog alarm

states.name: HH_SP
states.description: The Hi Hi Setpoint for the analog alarm

states.name: HHH_SP
states.description: The Hi Hi Hi Setpoint for the analog alarm

states.name: L_SP
states.description: The Low Setpoint for the analog alarm

states.name: LL_SP
states.description: The Low Low Setpoint for the analog alarm

states.name: LLL_SP
states.description: The Low Low Low Setpoint for the analog alarm

states.name: H_T
states.description: The Hi Delay for the analog alarm

states.name: HH_T
states.description: The Hi Hi Delay for the analog alarm

states.name: HHH_T
states.description: The Hi Hi Hi Delay for the analog alarm

states.name: L_T
states.description: The Low Delay for the analog alarm

states.name: LL_T
states.description: The Low Low Delay for the analog alarm

states.name: LLL_T
states.description: The Low Low Low Delay for the analog alarm

states.name: HYST
states.description: The hysteresis value for the analog alarm

notes: Shows pin connections configuration for an analog alarm block with multiple setpoints, delays and hysteresis

ANY_FORCES.description: Provides information regarding the status of variable forcing within the controller

outputs.name: YES
outputs.description: True if any variables are forced

notes: If any variable is in a forced state within the controller, the Boolean output pin YES is set to True
The block indicates the number of forced variables within the controller on integer output pin NUMVARS
Combined and clarified the Precautions Before APP_STATE Deletion, Relocation, or Causing a Major Revision Mismatch

ARRAY_AVERAGE.description: Performs the average of the input array IN at each scan if the ENABLE pin is True. It includes in the average the element whose corresponding INDEX_ENABLE array element is True.

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Constant number of IN values to average

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements are included in the average

notes: If INDEX_ENABLE pin is attached to a scalar variable, then all elements of the IN array are averaged
Three average types supported: Linear (1), Average Linear Angle (2), and Average Cosine Phi (3)
For Average Linear Angle, accounts for 360-degree wrap-around
For Average Cosine Phi, IN(n) must be within -1 to 1

ARRAY_CALC.description: Performs array calculations based on a specified equation

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Number of element of the array to evaluate

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements are calculated per the equation

notes: Array inputs are at least size N or a scalar

ARRAY_COMPARE.description: Compares two input values and sets an output Boolean as the result

inputs.name: IN1
inputs.description: First input array of at least size N

inputs.name: IN2
inputs.description: Second input - either array of at least size N or scalar

notes: Input variable data type is implied by the block data type
IN2 can be either array or scalar
Supports comparison functions: GT (Greater than), GE (Greater than or equal), EQ (Equal), NE (Not equal), LT (Less than), LE (Less than or equal)

COMPARE_1.description: Compares two input values and outputs true when IN1 > IN2
notes: Has hysteresis and sensitivity settings to prevent toggling around boundary conditions
Sensitivity defines tolerance band for equality
Hysteresis requires exceeding normal specification by HYST amount to change from True to False

GEQ.description: Compares two inputs IN1 and IN2, outputs 1 when IN1 ≥ IN2, otherwise outputs 0

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True (1) when IN1 ≥ IN2, False (0) otherwise

notes: Block has two timing diagrams showing behavior for 'Greater Than or Equal To' and 'Equal To' conditions
Output transitions are shown for both increasing and decreasing IN1 values

NEQ.description: Compares two inputs and outputs true when they are not equal

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True when IN1 ≠ IN2, false otherwise

notes: Supports comparing variables of any data type but both inputs must be same type
Output transitions from 0 to 1 when inputs become unequal
Output transitions from 1 to 0 when inputs become equal

LE.description: Compares two input values (of any numeric data type) to determine if IN1 is less than or equal to IN2. The result of the comparison is the output, OUT.

inputs.name: HYST
inputs.description: Hysteresis value to prevent toggling around boundary conditions

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True when IN1 is less than or equal to IN2 + SENS, False when relationship exceeds hysteresis

notes: Hysteresis and sensitivity settings prevent toggling around boundary conditions
Output becomes True when IN1 <= IN2 + SENS
Output will not become False until relationship exceeds hysteresis value

LT.description: Compares the two input values (for any numeric data type) to determine if IN1 is less than IN2. The result of the comparison is the output, OUT.

inputs.name: IN2
inputs.description: Second input value to compare against

inputs.name: HYST
inputs.description: Hysteresis input value to prevent toggling around boundary condition

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True when IN1 is less than IN2, considering hysteresis

notes: Hysteresis is provided to prevent toggling around a boundary condition
The less than relationship evaluates to True when IN1 is less than IN2
The block output will not become False until the less than relationship exceeds the value of the hysteresis input, HYST

ARRAY_COMPRESS.description: Packs up Boolean input arrays into a single integer array variable

inputs.name: IN0
inputs.description: Contains the first input (least significant binary digit)

inputs.name: IN15
inputs.description: Contains the sixteenth input (most significant binary digit)

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements are included in the compress

notes: This is a rubber block that can accept a maximum of 16 inputs
Input IN0-IN15 may be either a Boolean array or a single Boolean value
If the input is a single Boolean, its value is used when compressing for all array indexes
ARRAY_EXPAND block performs the opposite operation of the ARRAY_COMPRESS block

ARRAY_COMPRESS_UDI.description: Packs up Boolean input arrays into a single integer array variable. Compresses Boolean inputs into packed integer values where LSB is IN0 and MSB is IN31.

inputs.name: IN0
inputs.description: Contains the first input (least significant binary digit)

inputs.name: IN31
inputs.description: Contains the thirty-second input (most significant binary digit)

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements are included in the compress

notes: This is a rubber block that can accept a maximum of 32 inputs
The Array Expand Integer Long Integer (ARRAY_EXPAND_UDI) block performs the opposite operation

ARRAY_EMPTY.description: Empties array elements into individual output pins. Works in reverse of ARRAY_FILL block.

inputs.name: IN_INDEX
inputs.description: Offset in input array to start copy

notes: Block supports all data types
Array must be connected to input array and data type must match output pins
Expandable up to 32 outputs
When IN_INDEX is 0 (default), copy starts at offset 0
Block checks if array is big enough when IN_INDEX > 0
IN_INDEX should be less than (length of IN_ARRAY minus number of output pins) for copy to occur

ARRAY_EXPAND.description: Transforms the unsigned integer input array into its binary equivalent form and stores the expanded information into 16 Boolean output arrays.

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements are included in the expansion

outputs.name: OUT0
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (least significant binary digit)

outputs.name: OUT9
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (tenth least significant binary digit)

outputs.name: OUTA
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (eleventh least significant binary digit)

outputs.name: OUTF
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (most significant binary digit)

notes: The Array Compress Boolean (ARRAY_COMPRESS) block performs the opposite operation of the ARRAY_EXPAND block
For example, if the value of the input for a particular index is 5, the output Booleans contain the values 0000 0000 0000 0101 at the same array index
The least significant output bit is pin OUT0(x) and the most significant output bit is pin OUT F(x)

ARRAY_EXPAND_UDI.description: Transforms the unsigned integer input array into its binary equivalent form and stores the expanded information into 32 Boolean output arrays

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Input array value to be expanded

outputs.name: OUT0-OUT1F
outputs.description: 32 Boolean output values representing the binary expansion of the input

notes: For example, if the value of the input for a particular index is 5, the output Booleans contain the values 0000 0000 0000 0101 at the same array index
The least significant output bit is pin OUT0(x) and the most significant output bit is pin OUT1F(x)
The Array Compress Boolean Long Integer (ARRAY_COMPRESS_UDI) block performs the opposite operation of the ARRAY_EXPAND_UDI block

EXPAND.description: Transforms the unsigned integer input into its binary equivalent form and stores the expanded information into 16 simple, Boolean outputs

outputs.name: OUT0
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (least significant binary digit)

outputs.name: OUT9
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (tenth least significant binary digit)

outputs.name: OUTA
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (eleventh least significant binary digit)

outputs.name: OUTF
outputs.description: Contains the results of the expansion process (most significant binary digit)

notes: The Compress Boolean (COMPRESS) block performs the opposite operation of the EXPAND block
Example: if input value is 5, output Booleans contain 0000 0000 0000 0101

ARRAY_FILL.description: Fills an output array with input values

inputs.name: OUT_INDEX
inputs.description: Offset to start copy in output array

notes: This block supports all data types
An array must be connected to the output
The data type of the array must be same as input pins
When OUT_INDEX is 0, array copy starts at 0 offset
Block checks if array is big enough when OUT_INDEX > 0
OUT_INDEX should be less than [Length of OUT_ARRAY minus Number of input pins]

ARRAY_FIND.description: Determines if a given value is present in a given array and reports a Boolean status as to whether the value is found in the array. If found, reports the index of the array element containing the value.

inputs.name: IN_INDEX
inputs.description: Integer zero-based array index to begin searching from (default of 0, or beginning of the array). This should be less than the size of the array. If this value is greater than the size of the array, then the search will begin from the beginning of the array.

inputs.name: IN_VALUE
inputs.description: Value to be searched for in the array

inputs.name: SENS
inputs.description: Sensitivity value. This is not applicable for the Boolean block data type. If SENS is less than 0, it will be treated as 0.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Result of the find. True indicates that the value was found in the array.

outputs.name: OUT_INDEX
outputs.description: Integer zero-based array index of the array element that matched the value.

notes: If multiple array elements contain the given value, the block returns the index of the first matching element
Supports sensitivity input to be used when evaluating equality

ARRAY_MINMAX.description: Finds the location and value of the minimum or maximum element in a given array

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Number of elements in input array IN

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements determine MAX/MIN

inputs.name: FUNC
inputs.description: Choice of operation ( 0 for Maximum, 1 for Minimum )

outputs.name: INDEX
outputs.description: Integer Zero-Based array Index for Min/Max Element (1st instance)

notes: There is only one failure mode for this block, the failure to properly specify an input array (has either more or less elements than defined by the N).
The input N is an immediate pin and cannot be changed while the controller is online.

ARRAY_RUNG.description: Solves a Boolean equation of up to 16 input arrays. The equation is specified as a character string or configured through the Relay Ladder Logic editor.

inputs.name: A
inputs.description: Required boolean input used in equation

inputs.name: P
inputs.description: Boolean input used in the equation

notes: The equation may consist of up to 128 elements
Supported operators include: Parenthesis (/), NOT, OR (+), AND (*)
Arrays are of size at least N

ARRAY_SUM.description: Performs a summation of all the elements of the input array that have a corresponding INDEX_ENABLE array element set to True

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Constant number of IN values to be summed

inputs.name: INDEX_ENABLE
inputs.description: Defines which array elements are included in the summation

AVRG.description: Performs a rolling average on the last N values of the input IN at each scan

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Last input to be included in the rolling average

inputs.name: PRESET
inputs.description: True to initialize all N values of ARRAY to current value of IN

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Constant number of IN values to average

outputs.name: ARRAY[N]
outputs.description: Storage for last N values of input IN. This array must be defined with at least N elements

notes: ARRAY[N] must be defined with at least N elements
ARRAY[N] can be initialized to the current value of IN by setting the PRESET pin to True
The block automatically performs a PRESET if the block detects that the value of N has changed after a download
This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled

BIT_CNTR.description: Counts the number of Boolean inputs that are currently set to 1

outputs.name: COUNT
outputs.description: Number of input bits set to True

notes: This is a rubber block
Can handle from 1 to 32 input Boolean variables

BOOLEAN_ALARM.description: Configures alarm behavior for boolean variables

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Connect to a Boolean variable to document its alarm configuration

inputs.name: Unshelve
inputs.description: When set to True when the alarm is shelved, will cause the alarm to unshelve

notes: Unshelve input is available only when the Alarm Shelving property is set to Enabled
Alarm On Zero = False
Auto Reset = Disabled
Alarm Shelving = Enabled

BSORT.description: Block sorts the input array, SRC[ ], of data and transfers it to the sorted array, DEST[ ]. BSORT is optimized for small variation in the input data.

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Number of elements in the array to be sorted

outputs.name: INDEX[]
outputs.description: The mapping used to take the source array to the destination array

notes: The algorithm checks the array to make sure it is sorted using a comparison system and corrects any errors using a bubble sort algorithm
The number of elements sorted is the least of N, the array size of SRC[ ], the array size of DEST[ ], or the array size of INDEX[ ]
The function is complete when the list is navigated completely without an exchange of data between elements of DEST[ ]

CALC.description: A variant block that supports multiple data types for calculations
notes: This appears to be a configuration/instruction page rather than a full block definition
Shows how to change data type for variant blocks
Supported data types shown include: REAL, UDINT, UINT, LREAL, DINT, INT

STATUS_MONITORING.description: Block that propagates quality status if status operations are enabled
notes: When using division operator (/), if denominator = 0, denominator replaced by 3.4e-38, status will be error if numerator > 0
When using modulus operator (%), if divisor = 0, divisor replaced by 3.4e-38, status will be error if numerator > 0
When using RECIP() function, if input operand A = 0, replaced by 3.4e-38
When using SQR() function with negative input, output will be square root of absolute value
When using TAN() function, if input operand A equals to nπ + π/2, then OUT = 0
When using LOG() function, if input ≤ 0, output set to 0
When using LN() function, if input ≤ 0, output set to 0
When using power operator (^), if A=0 and B not integer, calculation uses rounded value of B

CAPTURE.description: Handles data collection with configurable enabling and triggering mechanisms

inputs.name: FMULT
inputs.description: Sample period multiplier for collection rate control

notes: Collections can be enabled manually through blockware or automatically
Manual enabling requires toggling ENA_CAP to False then True
Automatic enabling occurs when buffer is unlocked
Block re-enables capture after at least one upload and no new uploads within XTOUT seconds
NORMAL and CIRCULAR modes use FMULT to collect samples at slower rates
Sample collection occurs every FMULT scans
Trigger sample timing may vary when FMULT is not equal to one

DATESAM.description: Block for controlling data sampling and capture based on trigger conditions

inputs.name: TR_TYPE
inputs.description: Determines the trigger sensitivity and evaluation type

inputs.name: AUTOENA
inputs.description: Enables automatic re-enabling after buffer upload

inputs.name: ATOUT
inputs.description: Automatic enable timeout period in seconds

inputs.name: BUF_CTL
inputs.description: Controls how and when data is collected

inputs.name: ENA_CAP
inputs.description: Manual enable pin for data capture control

inputs.name: FMULT
inputs.description: Sample period multiplier for controlling collection rate

inputs.name: POSTSAM
inputs.description: Number of samples to collect after trigger event

inputs.name: PRE_SAM
inputs.description: Number of samples to collect before trigger event

notes: Buffer control modes include NORMAL, CIRCULAR, and ONLY
Trigger types can be Edge or Level
FMULT of zero is treated as one Frame multiple
Automatic re-enabling ignores manual enable inputs
Updated STATE pin Description in the States table

CLAMP.description: Restricts the output to values defined by MIN and MAX. Outputs IN_MIN or IN_MAX become True to indicate a low or high limitation active.
notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled
Block status modifications: OUT status is propagated from IN status. The status of OUT is modified to include HIGH_LIMITED or LOW_LIMITED when applicable

CODETIMER.description: Calculates the time in microseconds between successive performances of the block. Used to determine execution time of blockware sections or task run frequency.

inputs.name: PBAND
inputs.description: Specifies time period for which no statistical data is collected

outputs.name: DELTA
outputs.description: Time interval between successive block performances

outputs.name: SWEEPS
outputs.description: Number of block performances since enabled

notes: Block functionality is not supported in the Virtual Controller
All outputs default to 0 except MIN which defaults to 4294967295
Can be used to measure task scan period or performance time of block groups
Can calculate average performance time when connected to FIR block

PERFMON.description: Block for monitoring and collecting performance statistics

inputs.name: PBAND
inputs.description: Minimum time of sweep in microseconds to collect statistics on

outputs.name: MAX
outputs.description: The longest period in microseconds between block performances

outputs.name: MIN
outputs.description: The shortest period in microseconds between block performances

outputs.name: DELTA
outputs.description: The time in microseconds since the last performance of the block

outputs.name: NPBAND
outputs.description: The number of sweep times that have been longer than PBAND

outputs.name: SWEEPS
outputs.description: The number of performance cycles recorded

outputs.name: WORST1
outputs.description: The longest sweep time greater than PBAND

outputs.name: WORST2
outputs.description: The second longest sweep time greater than PBAND

outputs.name: WORST3
outputs.description: The third longest sweep time greater than PBAND

outputs.name: WORST4
outputs.description: The fourth longest sweep time greater than PBAND

outputs.name: WORST5
outputs.description: The fifth longest sweep time greater than PBAND

CMD_DISABLE.description: Conditionally disables the reception of commands from EGD CMP, Modbus Master, OPCUA, and Web clients

inputs.name: ALL_DIS
inputs.description: When True, disables commands of all types regardless of individual disable pin values

inputs.name: EGD_CMP_ERR_ENA
inputs.description: Enable error response for EGD CMP commands

inputs.name: EGD_CMP_ERR_VAL
inputs.description: Error value for EGD CMP commands

inputs.name: MBUS_NAK_ENA
inputs.description: Enable NAK response for Modbus commands

inputs.name: OPC_ERR_ENA
inputs.description: Enable error response for OPC commands

inputs.name: WEB_ERR_ENA
inputs.description: Enable error response for Web commands

notes: This block is supported in all platforms and in all redundancy configurations
Only one instance of this block is allowed per controller set
For each command type there is a disable pin and an error/NAK enable pin

COMMENT.description: Adds comment text in the block diagram and inserts a line break as viewed from the tool and on the printed page
notes: The comment will appear on the left side of the page on the next line after the previous block
The text of the comment is added in the Property View on the lower left side of the screen
The Show Description property must be set to 'true' to display the entire text of the comment
The COMMENT block controls the Mode (flow mode or list mode) of the diagram to the next comment block (or end of the code if there are no subsequent comment blocks)

COMMENT_BF.description: Adds comment text in the block diagram. Inserts a line break as viewed from the tool and printed page. Can control diagram mode (Flow or List) to next comment block.
notes: This block is not recommended for use in any new controller applications (ControlST V05.02 or later)
Show Description property must be set to True to display the entire text of the comment
The comment will display on the left side of the line on the next line after the previous block
Text of the comment is added in the Property View on the lower left side of the screen
Controls the mode (Flow or List mode) of the diagram to the next comment block (or end of code if no subsequent comment blocks)

COMMENT_NB.description: Adds comment text in the block diagram without causing a page break. The comment appears after the next break in connected blocks.

states.name: Show Description
states.description: Must be True to display the entire text of the comment

states.name: Mode
states.description: Controls flow mode or list mode of the diagram to the next Comment block (or end of code if no subsequent comment blocks)

notes: Comment text is added in the Property View on the lower left side of the screen
Controls diagram mode to next Comment block or end of code

COMPARE.description: Compares values and outputs boolean result

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True if values are equal, False otherwise

notes: Used to detect if heartbeat values are the same

GT.description: Compares two input values to determine if IN1 is greater than IN2, with hysteresis to prevent toggling around boundary condition
notes: The greater than relationship evaluates to True when IN1 is greater than IN2
The block output will not become False until the greater than relationship exceeds the value of the hysteresis input, HYST

GE.description: Compares two input values to determine if IN1 is greater than or equal to IN2. The result of the comparison is the output, OUT.

inputs.name: HYST
inputs.description: Hysteresis value to prevent toggling around boundary condition

notes: Output becomes False when greater than equality relationship exceeds hysteresis value
Hysteresis and sensitivity settings prevent toggling around boundary conditions

EQTO.description: Compares two inputs and sets output based on whether they are equal

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True (1) if IN1 equals IN2, False (0) if not equal

notes: Output transitions shown for both equal and not equal conditions
Can compare any matching data types
Timing diagram shows behavior with both increasing and decreasing IN1 values

<=.description: Compares two inputs and outputs true when IN1 is less than or equal to IN2

inputs.name: IN2
inputs.description: Second input value to compare against

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True when IN1 <= IN2, False otherwise

notes: Uses hysteresis to prevent output oscillation when inputs are close to equality
Output drops to 0 when IN1 increases above IN2
Output picks up to 1 when IN1 decreases below IN2

<.description: Compares two inputs and outputs true when IN1 is less than IN2

inputs.name: IN2
inputs.description: Second input value to compare against

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True when IN1 < IN2, False otherwise

notes: Uses hysteresis to prevent output oscillation when inputs are close to equality
Output drops to 0 when IN1 increases above IN2
Output picks up to 1 when IN1 decreases below IN2

CMP.description: Compares two inputs with hysteresis and sensitivity settings

inputs.name: FUNC
inputs.description: The comparison function (GT, LT, GE, LE, EQ, NE)

COMPRESS.description: Packs up Boolean inputs into a single integer variable

inputs.name: IN0
inputs.description: Contains the first input (least significant binary digit)

inputs.name: IN15
inputs.description: Contains the sixteenth input (most significant binary digit)

notes: This is a rubber block that can accept a maximum of 16 inputs
The Expand Integer (EXPAND) block performs the opposite operation of the COMPRESS block
Example: if input Booleans contain the values 0000 0000 0000 0101, the output integer will contain the value 5

COMPRESS_UDI.description: An expandable block of up to 32 inputs that converts Boolean inputs into a single unsigned double integer

inputs.name: IN0
inputs.description: Contains the first input (least significant binary digit)

inputs.name: IN31
inputs.description: Contains the thirty-second input (most significant binary digit)

notes: The Expand Long Integer (EXPAND_UDI) block performs the opposite operation of the COMPRESS_UDI block
Example: if input Booleans contain the values 0000 0000 0000 0101, the output integer will be 5

CTRLR_MON.description: Monitors controller health and status conditions
notes: ONLINE_FL_TMR = 5000

GEI-100682.full_name: Mark VIe Controller Standard Block Library Output Pins
GEI-100682.description: Defines standard output pins across different controller platforms

outputs.name: POWER_UP
outputs.description: True when controller is powered up and controlling, False when not in controlling state

outputs.name: SIG_FORCED
outputs.description: True when at least one signal is forced, False when no forced signals or reset after 2s when forced variables increase

outputs.name: SIG_FORCED_NUM
outputs.description: Number of forced signals in the application

outputs.name: OVERTEMP_R
outputs.description: True when R CPU temperature too high, False when normal

outputs.name: OVERTEMP_S
outputs.description: True when S CPU temperature too high, False when normal

outputs.name: OVERTEMP_T
outputs.description: True when T CPU temperature too high, False when normal

outputs.name: ONLINE_R
outputs.description: True when R Controller is Online, False when not Online

outputs.name: ONLINE_S
outputs.description: True when S Controller is Online, False when not Online

outputs.name: ONLINE_T
outputs.description: True when T Controller is Online, False when not Online

outputs.name: HW_ALARM_R
outputs.description: True when R controller hardware failure occurred, False when no failure

outputs.name: HW_ALARM_S
outputs.description: True when S controller hardware failure occurred, False when no failure

outputs.name: HW_ALARM_T
outputs.description: True when T controller hardware failure occurred, False when no failure

notes: Some outputs may be N/A (False) depending on controller platform

FAN_FAIL.description: Monitors fan operation status for R, S, and T controllers

outputs.name: FAN1_FAIL_R
outputs.description: R Controller Fan1 failure status (True when not functioning)

outputs.name: FAN1_FAIL_S
outputs.description: S Controller Fan1 failure status (True when not functioning)

outputs.name: FAN1_FAIL_T
outputs.description: T Controller Fan1 failure status (True when not functioning)

outputs.name: FAN2_FAIL_R
outputs.description: R Controller Fan2 failure status (True when not functioning)

outputs.name: FAN2_FAIL_S
outputs.description: S Controller Fan2 failure status (True when not functioning)

outputs.name: FAN2_FAIL_T
outputs.description: T Controller Fan2 failure status (True when not functioning)

notes: N/A (False) for UCCA, UCCC, UCSA, UCSBH1/H4, UCSC/UCSD platforms

MEM_LOW.description: Monitors RAM memory usage levels for R, S, and T controllers
notes: Monitors two threshold levels - 70% and 95% of total RAM memory

ONLINE_FL_TMR.description: Online failure detection block for TMR controllers
notes: Some outputs exist only when controller's redundancy is dual or TMR
Some outputs exist only when controller's redundancy is TMR

CTD.description: A countdown timer block that decrements a counter value
notes: CUR_CNT is only decremented when ENABLE is True
If ENABLE is False, countdown is suspended but CUR_CNT holds its value
By default, ENABLE pin is True
If RESET is True, countdown is suspended and value of CUR_CNT is set to MAX_CNT
MAX_CNT must be greater than or equal to 1
Once CUR_CNT equals zero, AT_CNT becomes True

CTU.description: Block counts up and accumulates counter value on rising edge of INC input

Does not cause potential voter disagreement diagnostic in packs unlike ControllerStateHeartbeat_R/S/T

DIV.description: Divides two numeric values; the quotient, OUT, and the mantissa, M, are outputs of the block

Block propagates quality status if status operations are enabled

The status of M always follows the status of OUT

inputs.name: ROWS_A

inputs.name: COLS_A
inputs.description: Number of columns in input matrix

outputs.name: ROWS_B
outputs.description: Number of rows in output matrix

outputs.name: COLS_B
outputs.description: Number of columns in output matrix

notes: Matrix input pins specifying the size of the matrix are immediate pins and cannot be changed while the controller is online
The indices defining the column and row start and end locations are zero based
Two-dimensional arrays (matrices) are collapsed into one dimension column-wise

SUB_MTX.description: Selects a sub-matrix from an input matrix based on row and column indices

inputs.name: ROWS_A

inputs.name: COLS_A

inputs.name: A

inputs.name: ROW_ST

inputs.name: ROW_INC

inputs.name: ROW_END

inputs.name: COL_ST

inputs.name: COL_INC

inputs.name: COL_END

outputs.name: ROWS_B

outputs.name: COLS_B
outputs.description: Number of columns in the output matrix

outputs.name: B

MSVCH.description: Selects output matrix from two equally sized input matrices based on input condition flag

inputs.name: B_FLAG
inputs.description: Matrix Selection Flag. If B_FLAG is True, C is set to A. If B_FLAG is False, C is set to B

inputs.name: ROWS_A
inputs.description: Number of rows in the first input matrix

inputs.name: COLS_A

inputs.name: ROWS_B

inputs.name: COLS_B

outputs.name: COLS_C

outputs.name: C[]

notes: The two-dimensional matrices are collapsed into one dimension
Matrix dimensions must be positive
The 2 input matrices must be equal size
Input array size must match defined rows and columns

MTRN.description: Computes the transpose of a given matrix

inputs.name: COLS_A

outputs.name: B

notes: In MatrixS notation, two-dimensional arrays (matrices) are collapsed into one dimension

Error codes 1-6 indicate various Modbus slave errors

MBUS_READ_1.description: Reads data from a Modbus slave device and stores it in the RD_DATA array
notes: The RD_DATA array is UINT type and may need conversion for other data types
Use COMBINE_* or WD_TO_I blocks for data type conversion

COMBINE_SR_1.description: Combines array elements into REAL data type
notes: Used to combine UINT array elements into REAL type
Used to convert UINT array elements to signed INT type

SPLIT_RS_1.description: Splits data from larger sized data types (REAL) into array elements

inputs.name: INPUT
inputs.description: Real input value to be split

notes: Used for splitting REAL/DINT/UDINT/LREAL data types into array elements for Modbus communication

I_TO_WD_1.description: Converts integer values to word format for Modbus communication

inputs.name: INPUT
inputs.description: Integer input value to be converted

notes: Used for converting INT values to word format for Modbus slave communication

MBUS_WRITE_1.description: Writes data to Modbus slave devices
notes: WR_DATA must be unsigned array
Data types other than UINT need to be split or converted using SPLIT_* or *_TO_WD blocks

MODSEL.description: Sets one of up to 8 logical outputs (MODE_0 to MODE_8) and clears remaining outputs based on a request from a logical input
notes: No two modes can be set at any one time
If two transitions occur simultaneously and both transitions are allowed, the transition to the mode with the lowest number will occur
The mode remains set until another input logical is set

MODEREQ.description: Block for selecting between mode 0 and mode 7

outputs.name: MODEW1
outputs.description: Mode word one indicates which mode is selected. Each bit represents a mode, with LSB being mode 0 and MSB being mode 7

outputs.name: MODEW2
outputs.description: Mode word two indicates which mode is selected based on magnitude of the word

states.name: SCRATCH
states.description: Pin that contains state information to be preserved during an online download

MOVE.description: Moves a source value to a destination variable

outputs.name: DEST
outputs.description: Destination where source value is copied to

notes: Used to transfer heart beat values between variables

MOVE_DT.description: Transfers the value of any variable into another long integer variable using truncation rather than rounding in the conversion

outputs.name: DEST
outputs.description: Destination variable (data type enforced by the block used)

notes: The data transfer occurs each time the block runs and the ENABLE pin is True
The analog variants of the block can be used to perform data type conversions as well as memory move operations

MOVE_IT.description: Transfers the value of any variable into another integer variable using truncation rather than rounding in the conversion

outputs.name: DEST
outputs.description: Destination variable (data type enforced by the block used)

notes: The data transfer occurs each time the block runs and the ENABLE pin is True
The analog variants of the block can be used to perform data type conversions as well as memory move operations

MULT.description: Uses multiple inputs to produce an output
notes: Part of Relational block category

NAN_BREAKER.description: Passes all good input values to the output. If input value is NaN, sets INP_IS_NAN to True and outputs the last good value. Outputs zero if there was no good last value.

inputs.name: INPUT
inputs.description: Input signal to check for NaN

outputs.name: INP_IS_NAN
outputs.description: True if input signal value is NaN

outputs.name: OUTPUT
outputs.description: Equal to input if input is not NaN, otherwise it is last good value

notes: Supports Real and Long Real data types
Triggers diagnostic message #356 if input remains NaN for more than four consecutive frames
The diagnostic message is not displayed if the block is run in the Virtual Controller. The rest of the functionality is supported.
Removed UDINT from the SRC pin Data Type
Updated the SRC pin Description
Updated the block description

NEGATE.description: The Negate (NEGATE) block multiplies the input by -1
notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled. Refer to the Status Monitoring (STATUS_MONITORING) block.
Value with status, if status option is enabled.

NE.description: Compares two input values (of any numeric data type) to determine if IN1 is not equal to IN2. The result of the comparison is the output, OUT.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: True(1) if IN1 is not equal to IN2, False(0) otherwise

notes: Sensitivity refers to the tolerance band of the equality relationship. IN1 is not equal to IN2 if it is numerically outside the band defined by IN2 ± SENS

HSST.description: Block for comparing two inputs with hysteresis sensitivity

ON_OFF_DELAY.description: Behaves as a switch with a delayed response whether turning on or off
notes: Passes a rising True value on the IN pin to the OUT pin if IN remains True for PU_DEL milliseconds
Passes a falling False value on the IN pin to the OUT pin if IN remains False for DO_DEL ms

PARITY_CHK.description: Checks for even or odd parity on input variables. It sets the ODD output to True if the input long integer contains an odd number of logical 1 and sets the EVEN output to False.

PREVOTE.description: Logic block for handling variable health and prevote destination variables

PID.description: PID control block with anti-windup protection and mode control
notes: Integration is calculated using bilinear transformation for trapezoidal rule
Output clamping prevents integrator wind-up when OUT is at limits
Derivative is calculated using three-point formula

inputs.name: LOCKON
inputs.description: Lockon control array of lockon codes corresponding to each mode

inputs.name: MODE
inputs.description: Operating mode, used as an index into the LOCKON[] array

outputs.name: ERROR
outputs.description: Error; if mode is PASSTHRU then ERROR is 0, otherwise ERROR is CTL_VAR - SETPT

outputs.name: LALM
outputs.description: Set to True if OUT is ≤ MINOUT, else set to False

outputs.name: HALM
outputs.description: Set to True if OUT is ≥ MAXOUT, else set to False

outputs.name: MSTATE
outputs.description: Mode state - stores the previous operating mode

notes: If D_ACT is False then KP' = -KP, else KP' = KP
Block uses double precision internal states for calculations

PULSE.description: Generates a Boolean one-shot of the specified width (WIDTH in milliseconds) at the output pin on the rising edge of the trigger

inputs.name: TRIG
inputs.description: Triggers a new pulse on its rising edge

inputs.name: WIDTH
inputs.description: Period of the pulse in milliseconds

states.name: CWIDTH
states.description: Elapsed time since the pulse began in milliseconds

states.name: ISTRIGGERED
states.description: State pin for storing the trigger status of the block

notes: Previous state of the trigger is stored with each performance sweep
The pulse width is always a multiple of the frame performance period
For accurate performance, set WIDTH as a multiple of the frame performance period

PULSE_EXT.description: Generates a Boolean pulse at OUT of the specified duration, WIDTH (ms)

inputs.name: TRIG
inputs.description: Triggers pulse timer on its rising edge

states.name: CWIDTH
states.description: Elapsed time since rising edge of TRIG in milliseconds

notes: If TRIG transitions from False to True, then OUT transitions to True and remains True until the time specified by WIDTH has elapsed since the last rising edge of TRIG
A specified pulse width of 0 will result in a one sweep pulse
The pulse width is always a multiple of the frame performance period set in the ToolboxST application
For accurate performance of the block, set WIDTH as a multiple of the frame performance period

P_B.description: Accepts Boolean commands from the Human Machine Interface (HMI). When input is False, output is False. When input is True, output becomes True and after duration WIDTH returns to False.
notes: WIDTH defaults to 1000 ms

PUT.description: Moves a value from a variable into an array element of the same data type

inputs.name: N
inputs.description: Destination array index (0 based, interpreted internally as unsigned)

notes: A block exists for each of the analog data types and is identified by its suffix
Data transfer occurs each time the block performs and the ENABLE pin is True
If N is out of range, the block transfers into the last element of the array

RATELIM.description: Functions as a rate limiter that limits the rate of change of the input signal

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Input signal to be rate limited

inputs.name: HOLD
inputs.description: When True, OUT maintains current value

inputs.name: PASS
inputs.description: When True, OUT equals IN (bypasses rate limiting)

inputs.name: PR_VAL
inputs.description: Preset value when PRESET is True

outputs.name: RATE_OUT
outputs.description: Current rate of change of output

outputs.name: RATE_LIM
outputs.description: True when rate limiting is active

notes: PASS takes precedence over HOLD
PRESET takes precedence over PASS and HOLD
RATE_IN is calculated for all cases
Rate limiting occurs when rate of change exceeds RATE_INC or RATE_DEC

RATE_LIM.description: Block that limits the rate of change of a signal

inputs.name: PASS
inputs.description: Passes input to output without limiting

inputs.name: HOLD
inputs.description: Holds output to current value when True

notes: Block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled
RATE_IN status is propagated from IN status
OUT and RATE_OUT status are propagated from either IN status or PR_VAL status, whichever is active
Statuses can include HIGH_LIMITED or LOW_LIMITED when applicable

RATEMON.description: Monitors the rate of change of input and sets flags when specified rates are exceeded

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Input value to monitor rate of change

inputs.name: HYST
inputs.description: Hysteresis value applied to rate thresholds

outputs.name: RATE_INC_HI
outputs.description: True when RATE is greater than 0 and exceeds RATE_INC

outputs.name: RATE_DEC_HI
outputs.description: True when RATE is less than 0 and absolute value exceeds RATE_DEC

notes: Uses hysteresis (HYST) applied to RATE_INC and RATE_DEC thresholds
RATE is calculated based on change in units per second of the input variable

RUNG.description: Represents Boolean equation logic for scheduling control
notes: Used as part of scheduling control logic
Shows three instances (RUNG_17, RUNG_19, RUNG_20) with different Boolean equations
Part of a larger 55-block scheduling system

ROTARY_SW.description: Uses the value of an unsigned integer input, IN, to select one of N Boolean outputs

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Selects which Boolean output will be True

outputs.name: OUT1
outputs.description: Contains the results of the selection process

outputs.name: OUT32
outputs.description: Contains the results of the selection process

notes: This is a ladder block that provides up to 32 outputs
The selected output is driven True while all other outputs are driven False
An input value of 1 for IN selects the first output, OUT1
An input value of N selects the last output, OUT(N)
If the integer input contains a value not within the range of 1 to N then all outputs are driven False

ROUND.description: Rounds an input value to the nearest rational number with specified precision
notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled.
Values with status are enabled if status option is enabled

SELECT.description: Selects output value based on input conditions
notes: If SEL1 is True then OUT = IN1
If SEL2 is True then OUT = IN2
If SEL3 is True then OUT = IN3
If no SEL inputs are True, then OUT = CASC

SELECTOR.description: Selects between two sets of inputs based on selection signals

inputs.name: SELA
inputs.description: When True Select Input Set A

inputs.name: SELB
inputs.description: When True select Input Set B

notes: Value with Status (Value only for Boolean block type), if status option is enabled.
Supports up to 16 input pairs and corresponding outputs

SBB.description: A ruther block that supports up to eight branches from a single Sequence Step Block (SSB) step, allowing parallel execution or selective branching

inputs.name: BR_ALL
inputs.description: When True, executes all branches in parallel. When False, enables selective branching

inputs.name: BRANCH1
inputs.description: First branch selection input (up to BRANCH8 possible)

outputs.name: NEXT1
outputs.description: Next sequence output for branch 1 (up to NEXT8 possible)

notes: Do not connect more than one sequence block to one NEXTx output of the SBB branch block
If a branch is enabled but nothing connected to corresponding NEXTx output, sequence will freeze
When BR_ALL is True, BRANCH1-8 inputs are ignored
In selective branching mode, lowest numbered branch is selected if multiple BRANCH inputs are high
ACTIVE and ON outputs are only on for a few scans of the task in normal operation

inputs.name: START[1:2]
inputs.description: Connection pin – from the NEXT pin of an SSB block

inputs.name: BR_ALL
inputs.description: If True branch all; if False selective branch

outputs.name: ACTIVE
outputs.description: If True the branch is active

outputs.name: ON
outputs.description: If True the branch is active and transitioning

outputs.name: GSTAT[7]
outputs.description: Global input and output connection to SMGR block

notes: Shows inputs and outputs for a branch control block
Appears to support up to 8 branches (BRANCH1 through BRANCH8)
Uses array notation for some pins [1:2] indicating multiple connections

SMGR.description: Collects global status information from all sequential step blocks (SSBs) in a sequence and distributes global inputs to each of these blocks

outputs.name: ACTIVE
outputs.description: Active status output - True if any SSB in sequence is active

notes: Must be in the same task as all SSBs in the sequence
Must be at beginning or end of task for accurate sequence information
Each SSB/SMB/SBB in sequence should have same GSTAT variable attached
For sequences with multiple parallel branches, all three status outputs (ACTIVE/ON/FAULT) may be True simultaneously
RESET input resets all SSB/SBB/SMB blocks in the sequence

SSB.description: A subblock that appears to be part of a sequence logic system

outputs.name: ON
outputs.description: Indicates the SSB is active and on

outputs.name: NEXT[2]
outputs.description: Pin to connect to next SSB, SBB, or SMB block

states.name: GSTAT[7]
states.description: Global input and output connection to SMGR block

SMB.description: Merge block with state machine functionality

inputs.name: MR_ALL
inputs.description: If True merge all, if False selective merge

inputs.name: RESET
inputs.description: If True reset the merge block (from any state)

outputs.name: NEXT[2]
outputs.description: Next output to the start input of an SSB

outputs.name: ACTIVE
outputs.description: If True the merge is active

outputs.name: ON
outputs.description: If True the merge is active and transitioning

outputs.name: FAULT
outputs.description: If True a fault has occurred

states.name: GSTAT[7]
states.description: Global input and output connection to SMGR block

SETARRY.description: The Set Array (SETARRY) block fills the destination array with the value set at the input.
notes: This block is a variant block that supports any one the following block data types: BOOL, Integer, Double Integer, Real, Long Real, Unsigned Integer, Unsigned Double Integer.

SQRT.description: Performs the square root function on the absolute value of the input

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Square root of the input value

notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled
If the input is negative, the output will be the square root of the absolute value of the input and the output status will be NOT_LIMITED- CONFIGURATION_ERROR-BAD

STATUS_COMBINE.description: Applies the value of IN_S as the quality status for the input IN. The output is the value of IN with status. If IN is value with status, then the status of IN is replaced by the value of IN_S.

inputs.name: IN_S
inputs.description: Status value to be applied to IN

notes: Using an invalid value for IN_S results in the status of OUT being NOT_LIMITED-BAD
Status functionality is always enabled for this block
This block propagates status
The data type of the OUT pin must match the selected block data type

SUB.description: Performs an algebraic subtraction: OUT = IN1 - IN2
notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled. Refer to the section Status Monitoring [STATUS_MONITORING].
† Value with status, if status option is enabled.

STATUS_SPLIT.description: Splits the value and status for an input with interface type (value with status). The value at IN is transferred to VALUE and the status of IN is transferred to STATUS.
notes: If IN is value only then STATUS will be NOT_LIMITED-GOODNC-[128]
Status regions: BAD if STATUS < 32
POOR if 63 < STATUS < 92
GOOD if 127 < STATUS < 156 or if 191 < STATUS < 228
This block propagates status

STEER.description: An expandable block that moves the input IN to one of the 32 outputs, OUT1-n, or to the cascade pin, CASC. Works in ascending order checking SEL1 to SELn, moving input to first True selection's output.

inputs.name: CLR
inputs.description: If True, all outputs and CASC are cleared

outputs.name: ACT
outputs.description: Block is active; a selection has been made by the block or CLR is True

notes: All outputs are set to zero or False for Booleans when not selected
If none of the selection pins are True then input is moved to cascade output CASC
If CLR is true, then all outputs and cascade pin are set to zero
Multiple STEER blocks may be cascaded by tying ACT pin to CLR pin of another STEER block
Highest priority selections placed on first blocks in string
This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled

SWITCH.description: Switches the output between two inputs, F and T, with a rate limiter. When SEL is True, the output tracks input T, and if SEL is False, the output tracks input F.
notes: Block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled
Rate limits do not apply to Booleans
When A_RATE and/or D_RATE equal 0, the output is set equal to the selected input
Output is rate limited when selection SEL remains constant and T or F change, as well as when SEL changes to select the other input

SYS_OUTPUTS.description: Provides a command interface between the application code and the I/O subsystem (I/O packs). Command input Booleans are synchronously transmitted to every I/O pack.

inputs.name: MRESET
inputs.description: Application Reset: Directs all I/O packs to reset any latched conditions resulting from previous trips.

inputs.name: RSTSUIC
inputs.description: Reset Suicide: Directs all I/O packs to unsuicide any closed loop regulators. Suiciding a regulator forces it into a safe state. Closed loop operation may not be re-started without lifting the suicide.

inputs.name: RSTDIAG
inputs.description: Reset Diagnostics: Directs all I/O packs and the controller to clear from the queue all diagnostics in the normal state.

inputs.name: RSTSYS
inputs.description: Reset System Limits: Directs all I/O packs to clear all latched system limit logicals.

notes: Only one instance of this block is allowed per controller
Commands are interpreted as level values by the I/O packs and should not be kept in the True state, but rather pulsed for a few frames and then reset

SCAN.description: Returns the most recent task scan period and the scheduled scan period in seconds
notes: TSK_TIM value may not be always equal to SCAN_RT if higher priority tasks preempt this task
The TSK_TIM and the SCAN_RT pins both hold the same value (scheduled task scan time) in the Virtual Controller
The actual task scan time is not measured

TEMP_STATUS.description: Determines temperature alarm conditions based on CPU and motherboard temperature sensors

inputs.name: OTPROT
inputs.description: Controls action when alarm condition is detected

notes: Only works with UCCA and UCCC controllers
Default temperature limit (DEF_THLD) is 90°C
Default hysteresis (DEF_HYST) is 5°C
When in ALARM, condition won't reset until temperature falls below threshold minus hysteresis
When OTPROT is True and alarm occurs, controller reboots into low power failure mode
Updated the Data Type for the UTCTIME output to UDINT

SP_F.description: A time-based setpoint function block that can trigger events on various time schedules and handles daylight savings time

inputs.name: SP_Fn
inputs.description: Enumerated function to select FLAG occurrence

inputs.name: HR_SP
inputs.description: Hour setpoint, based on 24 hour clock (9:00 pm = 21)

outputs.name: FLAG
outputs.description: Indicates that the set time has been reached

outputs.name: LOCKED
outputs.description: True if time is synchronized with an NTP Time Master

notes: Supports different time-based triggering modes: One_Time, Yearly, Monthly, Daily, Hourly, Minute
Handles daylight savings time transitions
Provides both local and UTC time information

TIMER_V2.description: Accumulates incremental time into CURTIME while RUN is True

inputs.name: RESET
inputs.description: When True, CURTIME is set to zero and counting is suspended

inputs.name: MAXTIME
inputs.description: Maximum time value for the timer

inputs.name: AUTO_RS
inputs.description: Auto reset flag that resets the timer after reaching MAXTIME

outputs.name: AT_TIME
outputs.description: Transitions to True when CURTIME equals MAXTIME

notes: The maximum value of CURTIME is 4294967295ms (0xFFFFFFFF)
Time period for AT_TIME transition is always a multiple of frame performance period
AUTO_RS provides timing intervals equal to MAXTIME except first interval which is MAXTIME minus one execution period
If AT_TIME output is connected to RESET input, timing interval equals MAXTIME plus one execution period except first interval

TOTALIZER.description: A timer block that can handle up to 128 sets of pins. Uses NVRAM to maintain critical application code counter values and accumulates rising edges of INCx pin in TOTALx variable.

inputs.name: INC1
inputs.description: Specified totalizer increments on the rising edge of this pin

inputs.name: INC128
inputs.description: Specified totalizer increments on the rising edge of this pin

outputs.name: TOTAL1
outputs.description: Output value of the totalizer for use by other application code

outputs.name: TOTAL128
outputs.description: Output value of the totalizer for use by other application code

outputs.name: ROLL1
outputs.description: Set True if the totalizer value has overflowed. The pin stays True until INCx increments again.

outputs.name: ROLL128
outputs.description: Set True if the totalizer value has overflowed. The pin stays True until INCx increments again.

notes: Only one instance of this block is allowed per controller in the application code
TOTALx is never negative
Uses special area of NVRAM to maintain counter values
Has 128 totalizers (counters)
When TOTALx reaches maximum value (2^31-1), ROLLx transitions to True and TOTALx is set to 0

TRNFUN.description: Provides a digital implementation of a second-order transfer function that can approximate phase lead, phase lag, simple lag, phase lead/lag, or bridged-T (Notch) compensator
notes: Can be used to create Butterworth, Chebyshev, and second order filters
Can be cascaded to form higher order filters
Performs stability check to prevent unstable transfer functions
Coefficient evaluation normally occurs in background
Performance penalty if ENA_DYN is set to True for foreground calculation
Accurate for frequencies < 0.3/T
For 32ms tasks, accurate up to ~10 radians or 1.5 Hz
For 2ms tasks, accurate up to ~150 radians or 24 Hz

Unknown.description: Implements a transfer function with second order numerator and denominator polynomials

inputs.name: ENA_FUN
inputs.description: If True the function is enabled, else the input value is moved to the output

inputs.name: ENA_DYN
inputs.description: If True the coefficients are re-evaluated in the foreground each sweep. Otherwise this calculation is performed in the background

inputs.name: DLOCK
inputs.description: If True an error found by the stability checker does NOT prevent the block from accepting new coefficients

inputs.name: PRESET
inputs.description: If True and ENA_FUN is True calculate the state variables so as to drive the output to the input value

outputs.name: CI_ERR
outputs.description: If True, indicates that the specified transfer function is unstable

notes: † Value with status, if status option is enabled.
Coefficients that result in unstable transfer functions are normally rejected
If DLOCK is set after discovering instability, coefficients must be altered and recalculated

TRAN_DLY_V2.description: Collects input samples each scan and stores them in an array. OLD is the oldest value stored in the array and OUT is the Nth value stored prior to the inclusion of the current input value. The delay time for OUT is equal to the scan rate times the least of N_DELAY or the array size of LIST[ ]. The delay time for OLD is equal to the scan rate times the number of elements of LIST[ ].

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Next value to be entered into the list

inputs.name: N_DELAY
inputs.description: Size of the array that defines the LIST variable

outputs.name: OLD
outputs.description: Oldest value stored in LIST array

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Newest or Nth stored value in the list as specified by N_DELAY

states.name: LIST[]
states.description: Storage array for the delay list

notes: Block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled
The array size of the variable connected to the LIST[ ] pin should be greater than or equal to the value of N_DELAY
The SLIST pin is an array pin similar to the LIST pin, but is only present on the status version of the block

UNIT_DELAY.description: Performs a one-frame delayed copy of the input, IN, to the output, OUT. The time period of the delay depends upon the Frame period with which the block is being performed.

states.name: STATE
states.description: Current value of the input (for feedback)

notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled.
Values with status are used if status option is enabled.

USB_HB.description: This block outputs the current value of the USB heartbeat counter. The block outputs the free-running counter value of the task in which it is scheduled. This indicates that the task is running as expected, which can be made available outside the task by attaching a global variable.
notes: The task heartbeat is anti-voted, so it can be used to detect a stalled task in a particular controller

The USB heartbeat counter should not be connected to an I/O module output. This generates an output disagreement diagnostic alarm. The DEVICE_HB block should be used instead to connect to an I/O module output.
Information is available as a local task variable _Heartbeat, which is a convenient indication from within the ToolboxST application

UTC_TIME.description: Outputs UTC seconds which is a LONG REAL data type. When LOCKED is True, UTC_TIME is synchronized with the NTP server. When LOCKED is False, UTC_TIME will display the Mark VIe system time.

inputs.name: LOCKED
inputs.description: True if local time is synchronized with an NTP Time Master

notes: The output is seconds lapsed since 1 January 1970 00:00:00

VAR_ALARM_STATUS.description: Provides access to alarm status information for the attached variable, specifically the acknowledge status and alarm queue status

inputs.name: VAR
inputs.description: Variable for which alarm statuses are to be examined

outputs.name: ACK
outputs.description: Alarm Acknowledge status for the input variable VAR

outputs.name: IN_ALM_Q
outputs.description: In Alarm Queue status for the input variable VAR

notes: If a variable that is not an alarm is attached to this block, the output statuses will return False
Refer to Mark VIe and Mark VIeS Control Systems Volume III: System Guide for GE Industrial Applications (GEH-6721_Vol_III) for more information on alarm subsystem

VAR_HEALTH.description: Monitors health status of up to 32 variable input signals and provides health status and link status outputs for each configured input

inputs.name: VARnn
inputs.description: 1-32 variable input signals that can be from I/O board, external network, EtherCAT, or UDH

outputs.name: HEALTHnn
outputs.description: Health status of corresponding VARnn input

outputs.name: UNCERTnn
outputs.description: Uncertainty status of corresponding VARnn input

outputs.name: BADnn
outputs.description: Bad status of corresponding VARnn input

outputs.name: LINKOKnn
outputs.description: Link status of corresponding VARnn input

outputs.name: LINK_UNCERTnn
outputs.description: Link uncertainty status of corresponding VARnn input

outputs.name: LINK_BADnn
outputs.description: Link bad status of corresponding VARnn input

notes: Input must be connected to either a VAR_HEALTH or AI block for health information to be viewable in live data displays
Each VARnn input has separate LINK status outputs even if sharing same link
Block provides both variable health status and link status for each input
Monitors up to 32 variable inputs

I/O Pack States.full_name: I/O Pack States Output Pin States

TMR_IONET_REDUND.full_name: TMR I/O pack Dual IONet Output Pin States

TMR_IONet.description: Block for handling TMR I/O pack with IONet network redundancy

outputs.name: HEALTHnn
outputs.description: Health status of the I/O pack

notes: Health status depends on IONet EGD Exchange reception
Link status indicates IONet network communication health

HEALTH.description: Block that monitors health status of inputs

inputs.description: Input variable to know the health status

VARSIM.description: When enabled, writes input values into output variables and sets the simulation flag in the variables quality field. When disabled removes the simulation flag from the variables quality.

inputs.name: PU_DLY
inputs.description: Pickup Delay - milliseconds to delay a 0 to 1 Boolean transition

inputs.name: DO_DLY
inputs.description: Dropout Delay - milliseconds to delay a 1 to 0 Boolean transition

inputs.name: IN1
inputs.description: The unscaled value to be written to the corresponding variable point

outputs.name: OUT1
outputs.description: A variable identifying the variable point to write

notes: Block is rubber block that may service up to 32 variables
For Boolean variables, uses PU-DLY and DO-DLY to specify transition delays
Can handle up to 32 input/output pairs (IN1-IN32 to OUT1-OUT32)

AnalogAlarmDefault.description: Configures an analog alarm with standard properties
notes: Shows alarm configuration settings for an analog input

OUT.description: Determines output status based on override and block states

outputs.name: OUT_TGL
outputs.description: Indicates if one or more inputs are in toggle-enabled mode

outputs.name: OUT_VAL
outputs.description: Boolean output status driven by the logical output of user-defined equation input

states.name: NO_OVR-NO_BLOCK
states.description: No Override and no Override is blocked

states.name: OVR-NO_BLOCK
states.description: Override active and no Override is blocked

states.name: NO_OVR-BLOCK
states.description: No Override and an Override is blocked

states.name: OVR-BLOCK
states.description: Override active and an Override is blocked

states.name: NO_TRACK-NO_BLOCK
states.description: No Track and no Track is blocked

states.name: TRACK-NO_BLOCK
states.description: Track active and no Track is blocked

states.name: NO_TRACK-BLOCK
states.description: No Track and a Track is blocked

states.name: TRACK-BLOCK
states.description: Track active and a Track is blocked

notes: BLOCK indicates operator has invoked toggle of logic driven inputs
NO-BLOCK indicates operator has not invoked toggle of logic driven inputs
OVR indicates override command is active
NO_OVR indicates no override command is active
TRACK indicates track command is active
NO_TRACK indicates no track command is active
When OUT_TGL is True, OUT enumeration contains BYPASS or BLOCK based on TYP
When OUT_VAL is True, OUT enumeration contains PERM, OVR, FORCE or TRACK based on TYP
All inputs have read-only external access
Block appears to handle up to 32 toggle-enabled inputs

OUT_TGL.description: Indicates if inputs are in toggle-enabled mode
notes: When OUT_TGL is True, the OUT enumeration contains BYPASS or BLOCK based on the enumeration chosen for the block by the input TYP

OUT_VAL.description: Boolean output status driven by logical output of user-defined equation input
notes: When OUT_VAL is True, the OUT enumeration contains either PERM, OVR, FORCE or TRACK based on the enumeration chosen for the block input TYP

RATE_MON.description: Monitors rate of change of input and provides rate flags

inputs.name: RATE_INC
inputs.description: Absolute rate of change increase setpoint (units/sec)

inputs.name: RATE_DEC
inputs.description: Absolute rate of change decrease setpoint (units/sec)

outputs.name: RATE_INC_HI
outputs.description: Increase rate of change HIGH flag

outputs.name: RATE_DEC_HI
outputs.description: Decrease rate of change LOW flag

notes: This block propagates quality status if the status operations are enabled.

Values with Status are enabled if status option is enabled.

GOODC.description: Represents status regions for good cascade conditions in various modes

states.name: LOW_LIMITED
states.description: Low limit good cascade state value = 193

states.name: HIGH_LIMITED
states.description: High limit good cascade state value = 194

notes: This appears to be a status region definition showing various integer values for different cascade states
Values range from 192-227
Includes states for initialization, requests, invitations, selection, local override, and fault states
Each main state (NOT_LIMITED, LOW_LIMITED, HIGH_LIMITED, CONSTANT) has variants for different conditions

HEALTHnn.description: Monitors the instantaneous status of variable inputs and provides health status indication

outputs.name: HEALTHnn
outputs.description: Health status output (True = Healthy, False = Unhealthy)

notes: Base health delay can be configured using ToolboxST application
Default base health delay is 3 periods
Supports simplex, dual and TMR redundancy modes
For simplex: False after loss of single packet
For dual: False after loss of both packets
For TMR: False after loss of second packet
All Ethernet links have expected bit error rate
Single packet loss is considered normal
Block appears to be a template for configuring analog alarm conditions with multiple levels

ALL_DIS.description: Controls disable state for all client write commands

inputs.name: ALL_DIS
inputs.description: When true, disables write commands from EGD CMP, Modbus Master, OPCUA and Web clients. When false, enables write commands.

UPTRI.description: Creates an upper triangular matrix with specified values
notes: Creates an upper triangular matrix with VALUE above diagonal and PREFILL below

LOWTRI.description: Creates a lower triangular matrix with specified values
notes: Creates a lower triangular matrix with VALUE below diagonal and PREFILL above

MISC.description: Creates a matrix with specified values in a defined region
notes: Indices are zero-based
If fill indices exceed matrix size, only appropriate entries are populated
Creates a matrix with VALUE in specified region and PREFILL elsewhere

BUFFER.description: Controls data sampling based on different collection modes

inputs.name: TSTAMP
inputs.description: Specifies if wall clock time in UTC format is collected with each sample

inputs.name: STATUS
inputs.description: Changes to Complete when sampling is done

inputs.name: FMULT
inputs.description: Used to modify block performance period for timestamp extrapolation

notes: Has three operating modes: NORMAL, CIRCULAR, and ONLY
NORMAL mode collects samples before and after a trigger event
CIRCULAR mode collects samples continuously until manually stopped
ONLY mode collects samples only when trigger event is True
TSTAMP is recommended to always be True for accurate timestamp information
Buffer can contain PRE_SAM + POSTSAM total samples
Collection may be halted prematurely in some modes

EQN.description: A string equation input that provides equivalent functionality to graphical AND, OR, and NOT logic blocks. Accepts a text equation that defines logic relationships.

inputs.name: INx
inputs.description: Logic inputs referenced in the equation

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Logic output based on equation evaluation

notes: Uses * for AND, + for OR, ~ for NOT, and () for grouping
Can be entered manually or generated from graphical logic
Automatically configures corresponding logic blocks when equation is entered
Bidirectional conversion between equation and graphical representation

GOODNC.description: This appears to be an enumeration table defining different status values for Good Non Cascade states
notes: This appears to be a status enumeration table rather than a functional block
Values range from 128-155
Includes states for block alarms, advisory alarms, and critical alarms
Each state type has not limited, low limited, high limited, and constant variants
Includes both active and unacknowledged alarm states

TR_COND.description: Defines the relational comparison condition between trigger variable and trigger value

inputs.name: TR_COND
inputs.description: The relational comparison condition (EQ, GT, GE, LT, LE, NE)

inputs.name: TR_VAL
inputs.description: The value compared against the trigger variable

inputs.name: TR_VAR
inputs.description: The variable against which the trigger condition is evaluated

inputs.name: TSTAMP
inputs.description: Enable UTC time stamp collection with sample

inputs.name: VAR1 to VAR32
inputs.description: One to thirty-two variables to be collected each sample

inputs.name: UPL_TYPE
inputs.description: Upload type setting (Manual, Automatic, Trip, Flash, TripFlash)

notes: Trigger variable is not required to be one of the captured variables
For Flash and TripFlash types, BUF_CTL must be set to NORMAL
For TripFlash type, both BUF_CTL must be NORMAL and CDL feature must be enabled
